pattern: A fixed-width sliding window over the second string compares character frequency counts against the first string's, sliding in constant time per step.
hints: A permutation of the first string is just any window of the same length with identical character counts.
Maintain a frequency count of a window over the second string equal in length to the first string.
Slide the window one character at a time, decrementing the outgoing character and incrementing the incoming one, and report success whenever the window's counts match the target counts.

edge_inputs.description: characters present but never adjacent as a permutation
pattern longer than target cannot match

stress.description: random target string with a small alphabet and a short random pattern
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abcde'
    s2 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(max(1, size)))
    p_len = rng.randrange(1, 6)
    s1 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(p_len))
    return [s1, s2]
stress.note: small alphabet makes matches likely, exercising both branches
